Each night except the first, wake the Tao Wu. They point to any player. That player dies—mark them with the [[DEAD]] reminder. Put the Tao Wu to sleep.

If the Tao Wu is about to die and there are alive Minions that are not marked with [[DRUNK]], [[POISONED]] or [[NO ABILITY]] reminder tokens, choose one of them and mark them with the Tao Wu's [[NO ABILITY]] reminder token. The Tao Wu does not die.
examples: The Psychopath is in play, and has already killed two players using their ability. Yesterday, the Tao Wu was executed but did not die, and the Psychopath instead lost their ability. On the next day, the Psychopath announces that they kill another player, but nothing happens. The town executes the Psychopath, who dies without playing roshambo.

The Tao Wu is executed. There are two Minions in play: the Assassin who used their ability to kill the Soldier, and the dead Cerenovus who kept their ability due to the Vigormortis' ability. Since there is no alive Minion with an ability, the Storyteller declares that the Tao Wu dies.

The Poppy Grower is in play, and the evil team do not know each other. At night, the Poppy Grower dies, and the Minions learn the Demon, who is the Tao Wu. The Tao Wu does not learn their Minions, due to their own ability.
tips: Not knowing your Minions is a serious handicap. Spend early days trying to identify your evil team through deduction and coded conversation.
Your death penalty for Minions deters your own team from selling you out — they lose an ability if you die.
Target strong information roles early. Without knowing who your Minions are, you can't coordinate closely — make each kill count independently.
If you can secretly identify yourself to Minions during the day, you gain the coordination advantage without the first-night reveal.
firstNightReminder: Do not wake the Tao Wu to give Demon info.
otherNightReminder: The Tao Wu points to a player. That player DIES. :reminder:

jinxes.reason: The Scarlet Woman registers as having no ability to the Tao Wu.
jinxes.name: Scarlet Woman

jinxes.reason: The Evil Twin registers as having no ability to the Tao Wu.
jinxes.name: Evil Twin

jinxes.reason: The Mastermind registers as having no ability to the Tao Wu.
jinxes.name: Mastermind

jinxes.reason: If the Tao Wu is about to be executed and the alive Organ Grinder has their ability, the Storyteller announces that no one is executed.
jinxes.name: Organ Grinder

jinxes.reason: The Tao Wu does not learn who the Marionette is. If the Marionette has no ability due to the Tao Wu’s ability, the Tao Wu learns who the Marionette is, but the Marionette learns nothing.
jinxes.name: Marionette

jinxes.reason: If the Tao Wu player changes character, any Minion that lost their ability due to the Tao Wu ability dies immediately.
jinxes.name: Hatter

jinxes.reason: If the Tao Wu player changes character, any Minion that lost their ability due to the Tao Wu ability dies immediately.
jinxes.name: Pit-Hag

jinxes.reason: If the Tao Wu player changes character, any Minion that lost their ability due to the Tao Wu ability dies immediately.
jinxes.name: Engineer

jinxes.reason: If the Tao Wu swaps characters with another player, Minions that lost their ability due to the Tao Wu ability do not get their abilities back.
jinxes.name: Snake Charmer

jinxes.reason: If the Tao Wu swaps characters with another player, Minions that lost their ability due to the Tao Wu ability do not get their abilities back.
jinxes.name: Barber

callout: If the Lunatic believes themself to be the Tao Wu, they also do not wake to learn any Demon info. If the Poppy Grower dies, the Tao Wu still does not wake to learn anything, due to their own ability.

name: Hun Dun
ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die. If a Townsfolk neighbor dies this way, good non-Travellers are poisoned until dusk.
creator: Gstone Games
quote: The beast is a four-winged bird, red as cinnabar fire and with six legs but no face. It can sing and dance, and it is known as Hun Dun.
lede: The Hun Dun waits for opportunities to poison the entire good team.
summaryBullets: If the Hun Dun kills a Townsfolk neighbor using its ability, all good players except Travellers are poisoned until dusk tomorrow.
The good team is made poisoned even if the Townsfolk neighbor killed by the Hun Dun is evil.
If the Hun Dun's Townsfolk neighbor has an ability that activates when they die, the ability is resolved before all good players become poisoned.
If the Hun Dun attacks a Townsfolk neighbor who does not die (due to being protected or already being dead), the Hun Dun does not make anybody poisoned.
The Hun Dun also poisons any good Minions and Demons.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Hun Dun. They point at any player. Put the Hun Dun to sleep. The chosen player dies: mark them with a [[DEAD]] reminder.

If the chosen player is a Townsfolk neighbor of the Hun Dun, all good players except Travellers are also made poisoned. Place the [[GOOD ARE POISONED]] reminder in the middle of the Grimoire. Remove the reminder at dusk.
examples: The Hun Dun is neighboring the Snitch, who neighbors the Flowergirl. At night, the Hun Dun attacks the Flowergirl. The Flowergirl dies and all good players are poisoned until dusk. The next night, the Pit-Hag turns the Snitch into the Mathematician, and the Hun Dun attacks the Mathematician. The Mathematician dies, and all good players are poisoned until dusk.

All good players are poisoned due to the Hun Dun's ability. The evil Cult Leader turns good due to their own ability, and is immediately poisoned. The next day, all good players agree to join their cult, but nothing happens.
tips: Choose your seat carefully. Sitting next to powerful Townsfolk means you can poison the whole good team when you kill them.
Use the mass poisoning strategically — on nights where multiple good abilities would fire, making all those results false creates maximum confusion.
Be aware that the poisoning is visible in effect. If too many players get obviously wrong information the same night, the town may deduce the Hun Dun is in play.
Your regular kills are unconditional — use them to eliminate threats regardless of poisoning opportunities.
otherNightReminder: The Hun Dun points to a player. That player dies. :reminder: If they are a Townsfolk neighbor of the Hun Dun, all good players (except Travellers) are poisoned until dusk. :reminder:

name: Qiong Qi
ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die and, if an Outsider died today, register as alive, and another player dies. [+1 Outsider]
creator: Gstone Games
quote: The beast resembles a winged ox, with spines like a hedgehog and bark like a hound. It eats humans, and it bears the name Qiong Qi.
lede: The Qiong Qi secretly kills more players when Outsiders die.
summaryBullets: If an Outsider dies during the day, the player that the Qiong Qi kills that night turns undead, registering as alive. The Storyteller chooses another player, who dies normally.
Undead players register as alive to other players' abilities.
Undead players are dead and have no ability. They are treated as if they are drunk or poisoned, waking as normal during the night but learning arbitrary info.
If an undead player "dies" again, their death is announced. They cease to be undead and register as dead from then on.
howToRun: While setting up the game, before putting character tokens in the bag, add one extra Outsider character token and remove one Townsfolk character token.
If an Outsider dies during the day, place the Qiong Qi's [[OUTSIDER DIED]] reminder token next to that Outsider's character token in the Grimoire.
Each night except the first, wake the Qiong Qi. They point to any player, then go back to sleep.
If there is no [[OUTSIDER DIED]] reminder token placed, the chosen player dies—mark them with the [[DEAD]] reminder.

If the [[OUTSIDER DIED]] reminder token is placed, the chosen player dies and turns undead (unless they can't die for some reason). Place an [[UNDEAD]] reminder token next to the chosen player's character token, and a [[DEAD]] reminder token next to another player.

At dawn, place shrouds on any character token marked [[DEAD]] or [[UNDEAD]], and announce that any players marked [[DEAD]] died in the night. Do not announce that players marked [[UNDEAD]] died in the night.
examples: The Mutant dies by execution due to their own ability. That night, the Qiong Qi chooses the Artist. The Storyteller decides that the Recluse dies. At dawn, the Storyteller announces that the Recluse died. Nobody learns that the Artist is also dead.
There are 3 alive players left and 2 undead players. The living Assassin is executed and dies. Since there are only 2 living players left, the game ends and the evil team wins.
tips: Encourage Outsider executions. Argue for executing Outsider-claiming players — each Outsider execution gives you a double kill at night.
The +1 Outsider setup means there's an extra Outsider in play. This makes Outsider executions more likely to happen naturally.
The UNDEAD player confuses living counts. Use this to delay the final day — if the town thinks there are more players alive than there are, they may not realize how close the game is.
Plan your double kill carefully — use the second kill to remove a powerful protector or information role.
otherNightReminder: The Qiong Qi points to a player. That player dies. If an Outsider died today, the chosen player registers as alive & you choose another player who dies. :reminder: :reminder:
callout: It is not very fun or interesting if the game ends early because there are a lot of undead players. When 2 or more players are undead, it is recommended to use the Qiong Qi's extra death to kill an undead player a second time, to make them register as dead.

name: Pauper
ability: Each day, you may publicly choose a player (not yourself) to pick a non-Demon ability: you might gain it until dawn.
creator: Gstone Games
quote: I've had a rough spell, but I can still work. Give me the opportunity to prove myself, and you won't be disappointed!
lede: The Pauper begs for abilities from other players.
summaryBullets: The player chosen by the Pauper cannot refuse to choose a character.
The chosen player must publicly choose a non-Demon character available on the current script.
If the Storyteller thinks the chosen ability would be too powerful for the Pauper, the Pauper might not gain the ability. The Pauper is not told whether they gained the chosen ability or not.
The Pauper does not change characters when they gain the chosen ability, and they lose it again at the next dawn.
If the Pauper gains an ability that activates on the 1st night, it activates tonight after all deaths have occurred.
howToRun: Each day, the Pauper may publicly choose another player. If they do, the chosen player must choose a non-Demon character from the script. If you decide that the Pauper does not gain this ability, nothing happens. Otherwise, if the character is in play, mark that character token with the [[BEGGING]] reminder token, or if not in play, replace the Pauper character token with the chosen character token and mark them with the [[IS THE PAUPER]] reminder token.
At night, wake the Pauper if they should wake due to the gained ability. If the chosen character is in play, decide who wakes first.
At dawn, remove the [[BEGGING]] or [[IS THE PAUPER]] reminder token, and put the Pauper character token back if needed.
examples: The good Pauper selects a good player, who chooses the Assassin. The Storyteller deems the Assassin ability to be too powerful for the good team at this point in the game, and does not wake the Pauper to use the ability tonight.

It is the first day. The evil Pauper publicly selects an evil player, who chooses the Puzzlemaster. The Storyteller decides that the Pauper gains this ability, making the Fool drunk. Later that day, the Fool is executed and dies.
tips: As a Traveller, you're a wildcard. Choose abilities that give you information or protection — or abilities that the town desperately needs.
If you gain a killing ability (like Slayer), use it wisely — it's a one-night window.
Let the town know what you're borrowing. Transparency builds trust and prevents confusion when abilities fire from unexpected sources.
Watch out for exile — Travellers can be exiled by the town if they become suspicious.

name: Qilin
ability: On the final day, something good might happen to the most engaged player.
creator: Gstone Games
quote: 麟之趾，振振公子，于嘘麟典！
lede: Use the Qilin to encourage players to participate more actively and make the game more engaging for everybody.
tips: Play with full engagement from turn one — the Qilin rewards players who invest in the game, not just those who get lucky.
As the Storyteller, use the Qilin's benefit to create a memorable dramatic moment on the final day.
Good players should try to earn the reward through quality play — but don't let the reward become the focus. The game is the point.

name: Guide
ability: Each night, choose 1 to 3 players: you learn if any of them were chosen or affected by evil abilities tonight.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Guide learns if their passengers are plagued by evil.
summaryBullets: Each night, the Guide chooses 1, 2 or 3 players.
If none of the players chosen have been chosen or affected by evil players' abilities, the Guide learns "no".

If at least one of the players chosen has been chosen or affected by evil players' abilities, the Guide learns "yes". If the Guide chose more than 1 player, they do not learn which of them are affected.
The abilities of evil Townsfolk and Outsiders also register to the Guide.
howToRun: Each night, wake the Guide, who picks between one and three players. If any of the chosen players were targeted or affected by an evil player's ability tonight, nod "yes" to the Guide. Otherwise, shake your head "no". Put the Guide to sleep.
examples: The Guide chooses the Fortune Teller, the Empath and the Baron, none of whom were targeted or affected by evil abilities tonight, and learns "no".
The Pit-Hag turns the Cerenovus into the evil Dreamer, who chooses the Soldier. The Guide chooses themself and the Soldier, and learns "yes".
On the first night, the Pukka chooses the Goon. The Guide chooses the Pukka, and learns "yes".
tips: Focus your choices on players you suspect are being targeted by evil — a 'yes' narrows down who evil is acting on.
Choosing yourself is valid. If you get 'yes' with yourself in the group, you know an evil ability targeted you that night.
Coordinate with information roles. If the Fortune Teller gets a 'yes' hit the same night you also get 'yes' on them, you can confirm evil was probing the same area.
A consistent 'no' on the same group of players across multiple nights can help establish who evil is avoiding — which can be as informative as a 'yes'.
firstNightReminder: The Guide chooses 1 to 3 players. Nod or shake your head.
otherNightReminder: The Guide chooses 1 to 3 players. Nod or shake your head.

jinxes.reason: The Chambermaid can detect if the Guide will wake tonight.
jinxes.name: Chambermaid

jinxes.reason: The Guide learns if the Mathematician is affected or not, even though the Guide wakes first.
jinxes.name: Mathematician

quote: The boat is slow and difficult to steer. It is weighted by the stench of evil.
callout: The player killed by the Lil' Monsta only registers to the Guide if an evil player is babysitting Lil' Monsta.

name: Broker
ability: Each night, choose 2 alive players: if they are the same alignment, abilities targeting one will instead target the other tonight.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Broker acts as an intermediary between teammates, forwarding other players' choices.
summaryBullets: If the Broker chooses two living players of the same alignment, any other ability used to choose one of them will instead resolve as if the other player was chosen.
If the Broker chooses two living players of different alignments, the Broker's ability will not change the target of other players' abilities.
Players do not learn when their abilities have been redirected, and the Broker does not learn if they have successfully redirected an ability.
Only abilities that involve making a choice can be redirected by the Broker. Passive abilities are not redirected.
The Broker's ability only applies at night, and has no effect during the day.

If the Broker has successfully chosen two living players of the same alignment, and one switches alignments later in the night, the Broker immediately loses the ability to redirect abilities that night.
howToRun: Each night, wake the Broker. They point at two living players. Put the Broker to sleep. If the two chosen players are the same alignment, mark them with [[CLIENT]] reminders. When a player uses their ability to choose a player marked [[CLIENT]], that ability is resolved as if they had chosen the other player marked [[CLIENT]].
At dawn, remove any [[CLIENT]] reminders. [[CLIENT]] reminders are also removed if either player marked [[CLIENT]] changes alignment.
examples: The Broker chooses the Ravenkeeper and the Artist. The Demon attacks the Artist, and the Ravenkeeper dies and gets to use their ability.

The Devil's Advocate protects themself on the first night. The second night, the Broker chooses the Devil's Advocate and the Po. The Devil's Advocate chooses to protect the Po. The Devil's Advocate will survive executions tomorrow, and may choose to protect themself again on the third night, as their last choice was the Po.
The Broker chooses the Recluse and the Chef. The Imp attacks the Chef, and the Storyteller decides that the Recluse registers as evil, so the Chef dies.
tips: Link yourself to a powerful information role. If the Demon targets you, they accidentally kill the person you're linked to — and vice versa.
If you can identify two evil players, linking them wastes evil's ability on themselves.
Be careful about which pairs you announce publicly — evil will avoid targeting your linked pair if they know.
The Broker's ability works even for good players' abilities. Linking a key target for the Slayer redirects that shot.
firstNightReminder: The Broker chooses 2 living players. If they are the same alignment, mark both *CLIENT*. :reminder: :reminder:
otherNightReminder: The Broker chooses 2 living players. If they are the same alignment, mark both *CLIENT*. :reminder: :reminder:

jinxes.reason: The Broker cannot redirect the Lleech’s choice of host or the associated poisoning.
jinxes.name: Lleech

quote: Deals may fail, but loyalty remains.
callout: If the Assassin targets a player affected by the Broker, that player will die without triggering the Broker's ability.

name: Prefect
ability: Each night*, you learn if a non-Townsfolk (& non-Traveller) died since dawn.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Prefect knows if the dead were Townsfolk or not.
summaryBullets: The Prefect learns about all players who died today or tonight, regardless of how they died.
If any of the players who died today or tonight is an Outsider, Minion or Demon, the Prefect learns "yes".
If more than 1 player died today or tonight, the Prefect does not learn who among them are not Townsfolk.
The Prefect wakes even if nobody died today or tonight.
The Prefect is only concerned with what character type players were when they died, even if they change characters later.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Prefect. If a non-Townsfolk and non-Traveller died today or tonight, nod "yes"; otherwise, shake your head "no". Put the Prefect to sleep.
examples: On the first day, the Psychopath kills the Lycanthrope. That night, the Gambler dies to their own ability, and the Demon kills the Saint. The Prefect wakes and learns "yes".
During the day, the Gnome is exiled. That night, the Demon kills the Dreamer. The Prefect wakes and learns "no".
One day, nobody dies and nobody is executed. That night, the Demon kills the Barber, and makes the Barber swap characters with the living Artist. The Prefect wakes and learns "yes".
tips: A 'yes' after an execution confirms your nominated player was not Townsfolk — useful for narrowing the pool.
A 'no' after executing a suspected Minion strongly suggests they were Townsfolk, protecting them from further suspicion.
Your ability fires even when night kills hit non-Townsfolk. Track both day and night deaths carefully.
Pair your information with the Undertaker if possible — the Undertaker names the character, you confirm the type category.
otherNightReminder: Either nod or shake your head.
quote: Let the proceedings begin!

name: Cleric
ability: Each night*, choose a player: if you choose the Demon, you die & they are drunk until dawn.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Cleric sacrifices themself to incapacitate the Demon.
summaryBullets: Each night except the first, the Cleric chooses a player. If that player is not a Demon, nothing happens. If the Cleric chooses a Demon, the Cleric dies, and the chosen Demon is drunk tonight.
Drunkenness lasts until dawn, after which the Demon is sober again.
If the Cleric cannot die for some reason, a chosen Demon does not become drunk.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Cleric, who points to any player. Put the Cleric back to sleep.

If the player the Cleric pointed to is not a Demon, nothing happens. If the Cleric pointed to a Demon, the Cleric dies—mark them with the [[DEAD]] reminder token, and mark the chosen Demon with the [[DRUNK]] reminder token.
At dawn, remove the [[DRUNK]] reminder.
examples: The Cleric chooses themself, and lives. The next night, the Cleric chooses the Assassin, and lives.

The Cleric chooses the Shabaloth, and dies. The Shabaloth is drunk, and cannot regurgitate a player they chose yesterday. The Shabaloth chooses two players, but neither dies. The Shabaloth becomes sober again at dawn.
The Innkeeper protects the Cleric and the Mathematician, and the Storyteller decides to make the Mathematician drunk. The Cleric chooses the Imp, and lives. The Imp then chooses a player, who dies.
tips: You are a guided Demon-seeking missile. Use your information sources to narrow down likely Demon candidates before spending a night.
If you die and the Demon is drunk, announce it publicly — the town knows a Cleric was in play and the Demon is compromised tonight.
If you die and the Demon is still effective that night, it may suggest you hit a player who registers as a Demon but isn't one.
Don't be afraid to guess. A wrong guess costs nothing. A right guess cripples the Demon.
otherNightReminder: The Cleric chooses a player. If a Demon is chosen, the Cleric dies and the Demon becomes drunk. :reminder: :reminder:
quote: The way that can be spoken is not the true Way.
callout: If the Cleric chooses the Minion holding Lil' Monsta, the Cleric dies and that Minion is drunk until dawn, but the Storyteller may still choose a player who dies to Lil' Monsta's ability.

name: Tabby
ability: Each night*, choose a player: if they are a good character & die to an evil character tonight, you swap characters.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The crown prince was replaced by a Tabby, allowing him to survive.
summaryBullets: Each night except the first, the Tabby chooses a player to be its prince.
If the prince is killed by an evil character's ability the same night, the Tabby swaps characters with them.
If the player is not a Townsfolk or Outsider when chosen, they do not become the prince, even if they change characters that night.
If the prince becomes an evil character, they are no longer the prince.
If the prince has an ability that triggers upon dying (such as the Sage or the Ravenkeeper), they use their ability before swapping characters with the Tabby.
If the death of the prince is prevented for any reason, or if they die from anything other than a Minion's or Demon's ability, the Tabby will not swap characters with them.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Tabby. They point at any player. Put the Tabby to sleep. If the chosen player is a Townsfolk or an Outsider, mark them with the Tabby's [[PRINCE]] reminder.

If the player marked with the [[PRINCE]] reminder dies due to a Demon's or Minion's ability, wake the Tabby and show them the YOU ARE info token, followed by the character token of the player marked [[PRINCE]]. Then, wake the player marked [[PRINCE]] and show them the YOU ARE info token, then the Tabby token. Swap the character tokens of the Tabby and the player marked [[PRINCE]] in the Grimoire.
examples: The Tabby chooses the Fortune Teller. That night, the Ojo chooses Fortune Teller, who dies. The Tabby becomes the new Fortune Teller, and the old Fortune Teller becomes the dead Tabby. The new Fortune Teller wakes and acts as normal.
The Tabby chooses the Fang Gu. That night, the Fang Gu attacks the Plague Doctor and dies, turning the Plague Doctor into the new Fang Gu. The old Fang Gu does not swap roles with the Tabby.

The Tabby chooses the Tinker, who was made evil by the Mezepheles. That night, the Tinker dies to their own ability. Since the Tinker is not a good character, they do not swap characters with the Tabby.
tips: Choose players with abilities that would be devastating to lose — a Slayer who hasn't fired, or a vital information role in the late game.
You can chain-protect: if you swap into a powerful role, you can continue using that ability.
If the Demon figures out who you're protecting, they may avoid that player entirely — use this to create a 'safe zone' around key players.
Consider protecting yourself — if evil kills you, you swap out of being a less important role into whatever the Demon expected to neutralize.
otherNightReminder: The Tabby chooses a player. :reminder:

jinxes.reason: If the Professor has already used their ability when swapping characters with the Tabby, the new Professor has no ability.
jinxes.name: Professor

jinxes.reason: If the Tabby swaps characters with the Banshee, the Storyteller does not announce that the Banshee died.
jinxes.name: Banshee

jinxes.reason: If the Demon has the Tabby ability and successfully activates it, the targeted player becomes the Tabby, and the Demon instead gains the ability of the targeted player's previous character.
jinxes.name: Boffin

jinxes.reason: Each night*, the Leviathan chooses an alive player (different to previous nights): if they choose the same player as the Tabby, the Tabby’s ability activates, but the chosen player does not die.
jinxes.name: Leviathan

jinxes.reason: Each night*, Riot chooses an alive good player (different to previous nights): if they choose the same player as the Tabby, the Tabby’s ability activates, but the chosen player does not die.
jinxes.name: Riot

quote: How could my beloved consort give birth to such a freak!

name: Patrol
ability: Each night*, choose 2 good characters (different to last night): if both are alive, they can’t die tonight.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Patrol protects two players at once.
summaryBullets: The Patrol, like the Innkeeper, protects two players from death.
The Patrol only protects players at night, not during the day.
The Patrol chooses two characters, not two players.
The Patrol can only choose from Townsfolk and Outsider characters, and cannot choose a character they chose last night.
If both chosen characters are in play and alive, the players with those characters cannot die tonight. If one is dead or not in play, the other is not safe tonight.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Patrol. They point to any two good character icons (Townsfolk or Outsider) on their character sheet. Put the Patrol to sleep. If both of the selected characters are in play and alive, place the [[SAFE]] reminder token next to the tokens of the two chosen characters.

The [[SAFE]] reminder is placed (upside down) even if the Patrol is drunk or poisoned, or if they choose the character of a player who is dead or a character that is not in play, as they cannot choose the same character the following night.
At dawn, remove the [[SAFE]] reminder token.
examples: The Patrol chooses the Poppy Grower and Chambermaid, both of whom are currently in-play and alive. The Imp attacks the Poppy Grower, who does not die. The next night, the Patrol chooses the Patrol and the Empath, both of whom are currently in-play and alive, and they will not die that night.

During the day, the Moonchild uses their ability to pick the Patrol. That night, the Patrol chooses the Hatter and the Patrol, both of whom are currently in-play and alive, and the Assassin attacks the Hatter, who dies. The Patrol survives.
At night, the Patrol chooses the Fortune Teller and the Empath. Both are in play, but the Fortune Teller died last night. The Imp attacks the Empath, who dies.
tips: Alternate your protection between high-value targets. Evil will notice patterns and route around predictable guards.
If you know roughly where the Demon is planning to kill, pre-empt them. Protecting the player they're most likely to target wastes their night.
Don't protect the same two players two nights in a row — the rule prevents it anyway, but even similar clusters tip off evil.
A player surviving after being targeted by the Demon is a very strong signal — combine with other info to triangulate.
otherNightReminder: The Patrol chooses 2 characters. :reminder: :reminder:

jinxes.reason: If the Leviathan is in play, the Patrol-protected player is safe from all evil abilities.
jinxes.name: Leviathan

jinxes.reason: If the Riot is in play, the Patrol-protected player is safe from all evil abilities.
jinxes.name: Riot

quote: Lay the world's injustices to rest, and bring peace to uneasy hearts.

name: Courier
ability: Each day, publicly name a character: tonight, you learn if they are in play. If you learn “no”, you then lose this ability.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Courier delivers one question per day — and when the answer is no, the route is closed forever.
summaryBullets: Each day, publicly name a character. That night you learn if they are in play.
If you learn 'no', you lose this ability permanently — no more daily questions.
Your guesses are public — evil knows what you are trying to confirm.
The CORRECT reminder marks a confirmed hit; NO ABILITY marks when you've lost your ability.
howToRun: When the Courier publicly names a character during the day, note it. That night, wake the Courier and nod yes (in play) or shake no (not in play).
If you shake no, place the NO ABILITY reminder on their token. They do not wake for further nights.
If the Courier is drunk or poisoned when they receive their answer, the answer may be false — they may also lose their ability falsely on a 'no'.
examples: The Courier names 'Imp'. That night they learn 'yes' — the Imp is in play. They keep their ability.
The Courier names 'Baron'. That night they learn 'no'. They lose their ability — no more daily questions.
The Courier names 'Poisoner' while drunk. They learn 'yes', but it's false — the Poisoner may not be in play.
tips: Start with characters you're confident are in play — confirming a known script character wastes nothing and keeps your ability active.
If you're trying to confirm a Demon type, be strategic. Naming the Imp when the Demon might be an Ojo is a wasted guess if you get 'no'.
Your public declarations tell evil exactly what you know and don't know — use that strategically. Sometimes naming a character you know is in play signals false confidence.
Once your ability is gone, consider bluffing that you still have it — evil may act differently if they think you can still identify characters.
otherNightReminder: Either nod or shake your head. :reminder:

name: Zhen
ability: Once per game, at night*, choose a Townsfolk character: if they are in play, they become poisoned and die.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Zhen risks the death of a Townsfolk to confirm whether they are in play or not.
summaryBullets: Once per game, the Zhen chooses a Townsfolk character from their character sheet. If that character is in play, they are poisoned and die.
If the Zhen chooses a Townsfolk character that is not in play, nothing happens, even if that character was previously in play or enters play later.
If more than one player has the character chosen by the Zhen, only one player will be affected by the Zhen's ability.
If the Zhen chooses the Zhen, they do not die, as the poisoning happens first.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Zhen. They either shake their head no or point to a Townsfolk character on their character sheet. Put the Zhen back to sleep.

If the chosen character is in play, mark them with the [[POISONED]] and [[DEAD]] reminders. Announce their death at dawn, but not why they died. The [[POISONED]] reminder remains on that player for the rest of the game.
examples: The Zhen chooses the Clockmaker, fearing that it might be a Demon bluff. The Clockmaker is in play, and is poisoned and dies.
The Zhen chooses the Artist, which a Minion is bluffing. As the Artist is not in play, nothing happens, and the Zhen does not wake to use their ability in the future.
The Zhen chose the Village Idiot. Both Wang and Li are Village Idiots, and the Storyteller chooses that Wang is poisoned and dies from the Zhen's ability.
tips: Save your ability for a confirmed Townsfolk. Wasting it on a character that isn't in play is devastating.
If you're evil and the Zhen claims, know that they can kill a Townsfolk of their choice — neutralize them before they get to use it.
As the Zhen, consider targeting the most dangerous Townsfolk to evil's plan — a late-game Slayer or a confirmed Investigator.
Bluffing as the Zhen can deter nominations — the town may not want to execute someone who might kill a key Townsfolk.
otherNightReminder: The Zhen might choose a Townsfolk character. :reminder: :reminder:

jinxes.reason: The Poppy Grower cannot be poisoned by the Zhen’s ability.
jinxes.name: Poppy Grower

jinxes.reason: If the Zhen chooses the Ban Xian, the Ban Xian becomes poisoned and dies.
jinxes.name: Ban Xian

quote: Sweet as wine, poisonous as arsenic.

name: Consort
ability: Once per game, the Storyteller will break the rules for you. You privately learn what they did.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Consort has a favour with the Storyteller — one private act of rule-breaking, kept secret from all others.
summaryBullets: Once per game, the Storyteller will break the rules on your behalf in some way. You privately learn what they did.
The rule-break is chosen by the Storyteller — it could be granting you information, protecting you from death, giving you an extra action, or any other creative benefit.
You are the only one who knows what happened. The rule-break is secret from all other players.
The Storyteller may choose the timing — it could happen on any night without notice.
howToRun: At some point during the game, perform a rule-break on behalf of the Consort. This can be anything — giving false information to the Demon, protecting the Consort from a night kill, granting them a bonus ability use, etc.
Wake the Consort privately and explain (or gesture) what you did.
Use your judgment about when the rule-break will be most impactful or most interesting for the game.
examples: The Storyteller decides to tell the Consort who the Demon is — this is the rule-break. The Consort learns the Demon's identity privately.
The Storyteller prevents the Demon from killing the Consort tonight — the Consort learns they were protected by a special rule-break.
The Storyteller gives the Consort the Slayer's ability for one use — the Consort privately learns this and may choose to use it.
tips: Don't reveal what your rule-break was unless tactically necessary — its value lies in being secret.
You know something no one else does. Use that information to guide the town subtly without exposing your source.
If the rule-break gave you direct information (like knowing the Demon), be careful how you use it — revealing perfect information is suspicious.
As the Storyteller, aim for rule-breaks that are interesting and impactful without being game-winning on their own.

name: Thaumaturge
ability: On your 1st night, choose a number. On the night of that number, learn that number of in-play characters.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Thaumaturge learns a number of characters on a given night.
summaryBullets: On the first night, the Thaumaturge chooses a number, and on the night of that number, they learn the corresponding number of in-play characters (living or dead).
If the Thaumaturge chooses "1", they immediately learn 1 in-play character. If they choose "2", they wake on night 2 to learn 2 in-play characters, and so on.
When the Thaumaturge wakes to learn characters, they learn characters who are currently in play.
If the Thaumaturge is created mid-game and chooses a number for a night that has passed, the Storyteller instructs them to choose again.
howToRun: On the first night, wake the Thaumaturge, who gives a finger signal indicating a number. Place that number of [[LEARN]] reminders next to character tokens in the Grimoire.
On the night corresponding to the number of placed [[LEARN]] reminders in the Grimoire, wake the Thaumaturge and show each character marked [[LEARN]]. Put the Thaumaturge to sleep.
examples: On the first night, the Thaumaturge chooses "1", and learns that the Artist is in play.
The Vortox is in play. On the first night, the Thaumaturge chooses "3". On the third night, the Thaumaturge wakes to learn three not-in-play characters.

On the fourth night, the Pit-Hag transformed the Baron into an evil Thaumaturge. That night, the Thaumaturge chooses "3", and the Storyteller shakes their head and prompts the Thaumaturge to choose again. The Thaumaturge chooses "4", and learns that the No Dashii, the Pit-Hag, the Dreamer and the Sage are all in play.
tips: Choosing a later night gives you more characters but risks the game ending before you learn them. Night 3 or 4 is often a sweet spot.
You know N characters in play — cross-reference player claims against your list. Anyone claiming a character not on your list is lying or the game state changed.
Evil knows you'll receive information on a specific night. They may try to kill you before then — be cautious as that night approaches.
A high number like 5 or 6 characters gives you enormous information if you survive — potentially identifying both the good and evil sides of the board.
firstNightReminder: The Thaumaturge chooses a number. :reminder: If the number chosen is 1, show the Thaumaturge the token of 1 in-play character.
otherNightReminder: If it is the night corresponding to the number of LEARN reminders in the Grimoire, wake the Thaumaturge and show them all character tokens marked LEARN.
quote: We watch the stars to catch a glimpse of heaven.

name: Brush Holder
ability: If you die during the day, you learn a good player tonight. If you die at night, you learn an evil player.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Brush Holder learns a player and their alignment, depending on when they die.
summaryBullets: The Brush Holder only learns a player, not their character.
If the Brush Holder dies during the day, they learn a good player that night. If they die during the night, they learn an evil player.
If the Brush Holder is drunk or poisoned when they die during the day, but sober and healthy during the night, they still learn correct info.
howToRun: If the Brush Holder dies during the day, mark them with the [[DIED TODAY]] reminder. At night, if the Brush Holder is marked [[DIED TODAY]], wake them and point to a good player. Put the Brush Holder to sleep and remove the [[DIED TODAY]] reminder.
If the Brush Holder dies during the night, wake them and point to an evil player. Put the Brush Holder to sleep.
examples: The Witch curses the Brush Holder, who dies when nominating today. That night, the Brush Holder wakes to learn a good player.
The Brush Holder is killed by the Assassin. They wake and learn an evil player.
The Brush Holder is killed by the Pukka. As the Brush Holder was poisoned when their ability activated, the Storyteller decides to incorrectly show them a good player that night.
tips: Your death is your most powerful moment. Time it strategically — dying by execution near the end gives you a confirmed good player to shield.
If evil kills you at night, you learn an evil player. This is excellent intelligence — immediately communicate it (if possible before dawn) or ensure a trusted player knows to ask.
Don't reveal your ability too early. Evil will try to neutralize you before they need to kill, knowing your death gives you info.
The day vs night split is meaningful: choose how you'd rather die based on which information type the town needs more.
otherNightReminder: If the Brush Holder died today or tonight, point to a player.
quote: The red review is done. Double-check everything.

name: Prosecutor
ability: After your first nomination, learn your nominee’s character that night. Demons register as good characters to you.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Prosecutor learns about the accused, but overlooks the Demon.
summaryBullets: The night after the Prosecutor has made their first nomination of the game, they will learn the character of their nominee, regardless of whether the nominee was executed or not.
Demon players will be shown as Townsfolk or Outsiders to the Prosecutor.

The Prosecutor learns what character their nominee was at the time of nomination. If the nominee changes characters before the Prosecutor wakes, the Prosecutor still learns info as if the nominee had not changed characters.
After the Prosecutor has learned their first nominee's character, they lose their ability and will not wake up to learn the character of any future nominees.
howToRun: The first time the Prosecutor makes a nomination, place the [[NOMINEE]] reminder next to the nominee's character token. Run the nomination as normal.

That night, wake the Prosecutor and show them the character token marked [[NOMINEE]]. If the player marked [[NOMINEE]] is a Demon, show the Prosecutor a Townsfolk or Outsider character token. Put the Prosecutor to sleep. Remove the [[NOMINEE]] reminder and put the [[NO ABILITY]] reminder next to the Prosecutor.
examples: On the first day, the Prosecutor nominates the Widow. That night, the Prosecutor is shown the Widow's token.
The Prosecutor's first nomination is the Fang Gu. That night, the Prosecutor is shown the Drunk's token.
The Prosecutor does not nominate on the first day. That night, they do not wake up.
tips: Choose your first nomination very carefully — it's your only chance. Nominate someone you strongly suspect is a Minion.
You're blind to the Demon — if you nominate the Demon you'll get a false character. This is a known limitation to plan around.
Once you've learned a character, consider sharing it immediately — the information is one-time, so don't sit on it.
Nominating early means you get more time to use the information. Nominating late is riskier but may be more targeted.
otherNightReminder: If the Prosecutor made their first nomination today, show the character token marked *NOMINEE*. If a Demon is marked *NOMINEE*, show a good character token.
quote: With a clear vision, one can see ten thousand miles, illuminating even the most hidden of wickedness.

name: Actor
ability: Good characters are good, drunk & know each other. Whoever wins, loses & whoever loses, wins. You cannot lose this ability.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
quote: If we shadows have offended, think but this and all is mended, that you have but slumber'd here, while these visions did appear.
lede: The Actor conspires with the town to turn the tables on the evil team.
summaryBullets: During the first night, wake all good players together and show them the Actor character token. All good players learn who is on the good team and, consequently, who is on the evil team, but they do not learn who is the Demon and who are Minions.
If the game ends due to the good team winning, the evil team wins, and vice versa.
Except for the Actor, all players with good characters are drunk. They all know an Actor is in play.
Good characters cannot change alignment when the Actor is in play.
The Actor keeps their ability even if they are poisoned, drunk or dead. The Actor player cannot change characters.
Good Travellers do not wake to learn the good team, are not drunk and may still change alignment mid-game.
howToRun: After the Minion and Demon info steps during the first night, wake all players who are good characters (good-aligned Travellers are not woken) together. Show them the Actor character token if you want.
When the game ends, reverse the victory and announce this to the group.
examples: The Actor is in play. The game continues until only the Shabaloth and the Devil's Advocate are left alive. The game ends and the good team wins.
The Actor is in play. At night, the Vigormortis decides to kill themself. The game ends and the evil team wins.
The Actor is not in play. One night, the Philosopher chooses to gain the Actor's ability. All good players wake up together and learn each other.
The Actor learns the Mezepheles word and says it. That night, the Actor does not wake or turn evil. The next morning, the Actor pretends to have been turned evil to deceive the Mezepheles.
tips: All Actors know each other from night one — use this network to coordinate strategy without evil intercepting.
Good players are all drunk — their information is unreliable. Focus on deduction from actions rather than ability results.
The victory reversal is a powerful trump card. Let evil overcommit to their normal win condition while you secretly set up the reverse.
Evil must adapt entirely — their usual strategy of killing key Townsfolk and controlling nominations needs to flip.
firstNightReminder: Wake all Townsfolk and Outsiders together.
