name: Server
ability: One of your good neighbors (not a Traveller) is drunk, even if you die by execution.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Server keeps a neighbor drunk.
summaryBullets: The Storyteller chooses which good neighbor is drunk, regardless of whether they are alive or dead.
The Server's neighboring evil players are skipped. If both of the Server's neighbors are good Travellers, nothing happens.
If the Server dies by execution, the drunk player is still drunk. If the Server dies by any other means, they no longer cause drunkenness.
If a new player becomes the Server, or a drunk good player turns evil, the player who is drunk may change immediately.
howToRun: While preparing the first night, mark a good neighbor of the Server (not a Traveller) with the Server's [[DRUNK]] reminder. This player is drunk.
If the Server dies by execution, the [[DRUNK]] reminder is not removed. If the Server dies for any other reason, the [[DRUNK]] reminder is removed immediately.

If a good player made drunk by the Server turns evil, or the Server turns into a different character, or if a new player becomes the Server, a new good neighbor of the Server becomes drunk, and the old neighbor becomes healthy—move the Server's [[DRUNK]] reminder immediately if needed.
examples: The Server's neighbors are the Fortune Teller and the Sage. The Storyteller chooses to make the Fortune Teller drunk.
The Server died by execution today. The Undertaker sitting next to the Server is still drunk, and learns the Poisoner tonight.
The Empath is made drunk by the neighboring Server. The Demon kills the Server tonight. The Empath immediately becomes sober and learns correct info tonight.

The Mayor is sitting between the Chambermaid and the Server, and is made drunk by the Server. During the day, the Mayor uttered the Mezepheles' word. Tonight, the Mayor turns evil and becomes sober, and the Chambermaid becomes drunk instead.
tips: Try to sit next to players who aren't critical information roles — your drunk effect is fixed and you can't control which neighbor suffers.
If you suspect you're the drunk neighbor of a Server, your information is unreliable. Don't stake the town's strategy on your readings.
The Server's ability persists after execution — the town can't execute you to remove the drunk. This is important to understand when planning executions.
If you can identify who the Server is and who their drunk neighbor is, you can correctly discount that neighbor's information.
quote: Care for another drink, sir?

name: Embalmer
ability: If 5 or more players live and you nominate, execute & kill the Demon, you become the evil Demon. (Travellers don’t count)
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Embalmer sends Demons to rest, but turns into a Demon themself.
summaryBullets: If there are five or more players just before the Demon is nominated by the Embalmer and then executed, the Embalmer immediately becomes the Demon, and the game continues.
Travellers do not count as players when seeing if the Embalmer's ability triggers.
If fewer than 5 players are alive, and the Embalmer nominates and successfully executes the Demon, the Demon dies and good wins.
If the Demon is nominated by the Embalmer but is not executed, is executed but survives, or is nominated by another player, the Embalmer does not change character or alignment.
The Embalmer turns evil when they become the Demon, even if the Demon they nominated and executed was good.
howToRun: If there are five or more players alive and the Embalmer nominates the Demon, who is executed and dies as a result, place the [[IS THE DEMON]] reminder by the Embalmer, changing that player's character from the Embalmer to the Demon. They are now the evil Demon and have the Demon's ability.
That night, wake the new Demon, show them the YOU ARE info token, then show them the Demon's character token, followed by thumbs down for evil.
examples: Seven players are alive: the Imp, the Embalmer, two Townsfolk and three Travellers. The Embalmer nominates the Imp, who is executed and dies. The game ends and good wins, because Travellers do not add to the player count for the Embalmer's ability.
Five players are alive: the Imp, the Baron, the Embalmer and two Townsfolk. The Embalmer nominates the Imp, who is executed and dies. The Embalmer becomes the evil Imp, and the game continues.

Lan is the Embalmer. The Fortune Teller chooses Lan and Mei and learns "no". Later, Lan nominates and executes the Demon, and becomes the new Demon. That night, the Fortune Teller chooses Lan and Mei again, and learns "yes".
tips: Your ability is a double-edged sword — you can win the game for evil by executing the Demon, but you also allow the town to identify you as the new Demon.
If you successfully transform, the town knows the previous Demon is dead — but may not immediately suspect you. Use the confusion.
As good players, be wary of executing the Demon with an Embalmer in play — you may not end the game, you may just change who the Demon is.
The 5-player threshold means this ability is mostly mid-game. Late game, the Embalmer's trigger rarely fires.
otherNightReminder: If the Embalmer became the Demon today, show them the *YOU ARE* & Demon tokens. Give a thumbs down.

jinxes.reason: If the Embalmer executes and becomes the Demon, the Scarlet Woman does not become a Demon.
jinxes.name: Scarlet Woman

jinxes.reason: If there are two living Al-Hadikhias, the Embalmer Al-Hadikhia becomes the Embalmer again, but does not turn good.
jinxes.name: Al-Hadikhia

jinxes.reason: If there are 5 or more players alive and the player holding Lil’ Monsta dies from the Embalmer’s nomination, the Embalmer turns evil and is given Lil’ Monsta tonight.
jinxes.name: Lil' Monsta

jinxes.reason: If there are 5 or more players alive and the Lleech host dies by the Embalmer’s nomination, the old Lleech dies tonight, and the Embalmer becomes the evil Lleech but does not kill tonight.
jinxes.name: Lleech

quote: Unable to quell the hatred in my heart, I have lost the one I held so dear.

name: Vintner
ability: Each night, choose a Townsfolk character: their ability yields info of your choice next time.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Vintner decides what misinformation to spread.
summaryBullets: Each night, the Vintner chooses a Townsfolk character, and then tells the Storyteller what info that character's ability will yield next time.
The Vintner does not affect information gained by other means.
When a player affected by the Vintner obtains information due to their own ability, even if drunk or poisoned, they must learn the info the Vintner gave.
If the Vintner gives info to a character that could not normally learn this info, the Vintner's ability has no effect.
The Vintner chooses a character, not a player. If the Vintner chooses a character that is not in play, no one is given that piece of info.
howToRun: Each night, wake the Vintner and have them choose a Townsfolk character on the character sheet, as well as a piece of info (nod, gesture a number, point to a player etc.). Put the Vintner back to sleep.
If the chosen character is in-play and the information matches what they might normally learn, place the [[TIPSY]] reminder next to their character token.
Whenever an ability marked [[TIPSY]] would yield information, you must provide the information given by the Vintner. Then, remove the [[TIPSY]] reminder.
examples: The Vintner chose the Fortune Teller and gave the Storyteller a "yes". That night, regardless of which two players the Fortune Teller chose, they learn "yes".

On the first night, the Philosopher chose to gain the Town Crier's ability, making the Town Crier drunk. That night, the Vintner chose the Town Crier and gave the Storyteller a "no". On the first day, a nomination was made by a Minion. On the second night, the Philosopher learns "yes" and the Town Crier learns "no".
tips: Coordinate with your Demon — corrupt the information role most likely to expose the Demon this turn.
Your false information should be plausible. If the Empath would realistically get a '0', giving '0' is believable. Giving '3' when there are no evil neighbors is suspicious.
Vary your targets. If the same player gets wrong info every night, the town notices.
You can target a Townsfolk who isn't in play — the ability does nothing but you've spent your night. Choose wisely.
firstNightReminder: The Vintner chooses a Townsfolk character and a piece of info. :reminder:
otherNightReminder: The Vintner chooses a Townsfolk character and a piece of info. :reminder:
quote: It's just drunken ramblings; nothing to take seriously.
callout: As the Vintner's info is yielded by the Vintner's ability, it is unaffected by the Vortox.

name: Gu Diao
ability: Each night, choose an alive good neighbor: you learn their character, and they are poisoned & register as evil Gu Diao until dusk.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Gu Diao haunts a neighbor each night — learning their secret while making them appear monstrous to others.
summaryBullets: Each night, choose an alive good neighbor (left or right). You learn their character, and they are poisoned and register as the evil Gu Diao to all other players until the next dusk.
The chosen neighbor appears to be the evil Gu Diao to any ability that would identify them.
The chosen neighbor is poisoned — their ability gives false results while they are marked.
The GU DIAO reminder marks the affected player.
howToRun: Each night, wake the Gu Diao. They indicate left or right. Wake the next alive good neighbor in that direction. Show them the GU DIAO reminder — they now know they are marked.
Place the GU DIAO reminder on that player's token. They are poisoned and register as the evil Gu Diao to any ability checks.
At dusk, remove the GU DIAO reminder. The Gu Diao may choose again the following night.
Note: the Gu Diao's ability applies information interference only — non-information abilities are unaffected.
examples: The Gu Diao chooses left. The Fortune Teller checks the Gu Diao's left neighbor and another player — they learn 'yes' (the marked player registers as Gu Diao, an evil character).
The Gu Diao marks the Empath. The Empath's neighbor count is false because they are poisoned.
The Investigator checks the marked player and someone else. They learn the pair contains a 'Gu Diao' — correct, since the marked player registers as such.
tips: Choose the neighbor most likely to be questioned by information roles. Their corrupted registration misdirects the town's investigations.
The poisoned neighbor will give false info — but they know they're marked. Coordinate so you don't accidentally expose yourself.
Rotating between your two neighbors each night prevents the town from establishing a pattern in who gives bad info.
If the marked player realizes they're always giving wrong info, they may deduce a Gu Diao is nearby. Keep your distance social.
firstNightReminder: The Gu Diao chooses a living good neighbor. :reminder: Show the Gu Diao that player’s character token.
otherNightReminder: The Gu Diao chooses a living good neighbor. :reminder: Show the Gu Diao that player’s character token.

name: Meng Po
ability: Each night*, choose a player: if they are alive, they choose to 1) lose their ability, or 2) die & keep their ability until dusk.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: Meng Po offers her brew each night — forget your power, or keep it and lose yourself to the dark.
summaryBullets: Each night (not the first), choose a player. If they are alive, they must choose: lose their ability, or die and keep their ability until dusk.
The chosen player makes this choice privately with the Storyteller.
If they choose to lose their ability, it is gone permanently.
If they choose to die and keep their ability, their ability persists until the next dusk even though they are dead.
The CHOSEN reminder tracks who was offered the choice.
howToRun: Each night (not the first), wake Meng Po. They point to a player. If that player is alive, wake them. Show them two options: 1) lose ability, or 2) die but keep ability until dusk. They point to their choice.
If they choose to lose their ability, place NO ABILITY on their token. If they choose to die, mark them DEAD — their ability persists until dusk.
If the chosen player is already dead, nothing happens.
examples: Meng Po chooses the Slayer. The Slayer chooses to die rather than give up their shot at the Demon — they die but can still use the Slayer ability until dusk.
Meng Po chooses the Fortune Teller. The Fortune Teller chooses to lose their ability — they survive but can no longer check players at night.
Meng Po chooses a dead player. Nothing happens — the offer requires the target to be alive.
tips: Target the most powerful information role — making them choose between their life and their ability is devastating either way.
If a player dies choosing to keep their ability, use that knowledge. They sacrificed themselves to fire off one last ability — trust their final action.
Coordinate with the Demon. Killing a key protector on a night the Demon plans to strike removes two obstacles at once.
Be careful about targeting players near the end game — a forced death can accidentally trigger win conditions.
otherNightReminder: The Meng Po chooses a player. Wake that player and show them *THIS CHARACTER SELECTED YOU* and the Meng Po character token. They give a finger signal. :reminder:

name: Huapi
ability: On your 1st night, choose an alive player: they die but register as alive. If they die again, they live & you regain this ability.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Huapi possesses its target, moving to a different one when threatened.
summaryBullets: The Huapi kills a player on the first night, but is the only one who knows that they are dead.
The Huapi's victim is put in the "undead" state, registering as alive to all abilities.
If the undead player would die again, they are instead revived, and the Huapi selects a new target that night.
If the player chosen by the Huapi cannot die for some reason, they do not become undead. If they would later die, the Huapi still protects them from death and chooses again tonight.
howToRun: On the first night, wake the Huapi. The Huapi points to a player. Put the Huapi to sleep. The player chosen by the Huapi dies—put a shroud on their token, and mark them with the [[UNDEAD]] reminder. Do not announce their death to the town.

If the player marked with the [[UNDEAD]] reminder would die for any reason, they do not, but are revived—remove their shroud and the [[UNDEAD]] reminder and place the [[ON THE PROWL]] reminder by the Huapi.
If the [[ON THE PROWL]] reminder is placed, wake the Huapi that night and prompt the Huapi to choose a victim. Remove the [[ON THE PROWL]] reminder.
examples: On the first night, the Huapi chooses the Chambermaid, who becomes undead. The Chambermaid then chooses the Huapi and the Fearmonger, and incorrectly learns "0".
On the first night, the Huapi chooses the Soldier, who becomes undead. The Soldier is later attacked by the Imp and comes back to life, and the Huapi chooses another player.

On the second night, the Pit-Hag transforms the Baron into the Huapi, who then chooses the Monk. The next day, the Monk is executed and does not die, but is revived. The Huapi chooses a new victim the following night.
tips: Mark a player who looks like a Demon target — when the Demon hits them, it wastes their kill and revives an ally.
Your marked player is secretly dead. If they're an information role, their ability still fires but they're actually dead — don't reveal this to the town.
Regaining your ability on revival means you can chain this protection across the game. Stay active and keep marking.
The revival is automatic — you don't get to choose. Pick targets who would benefit from being revived at the key moment.
firstNightReminder: The Huapi points to a player. :reminder:
otherNightReminder: If the previously chosen player was revived by the Huapi, the Huapi points to a player. :reminder:
quote: How foolish are the people! Clearly demonic, yet regarded as beautiful. How deluded are the fools! Clearly loyal, yet regarded as false.
callout: If the Huapi chooses a Demon, the game ends immediately as the Demon does not register as alive for the purposes of ending the game. This applies even if there is a Scarlet Woman in play.

name: Warden
ability: Each night, choose up to 3 players. 1 player you chose last night dies: if any of them died today, all die tonight.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Warden keeps a list — and settles accounts the moment someone on that list faces justice.
summaryBullets: Each night, choose up to 3 players. One player you chose last night dies — but if any of them died during the day, all 3 die tonight instead.
On the first night, you choose your initial 3 but no one dies yet — the list takes effect from night 2 onward.
The kill from your previous night's list is in addition to this night's choice.
CHOSEN reminders mark your list; DEAD reminders mark this night's kills.
howToRun: Each night, wake the Warden. They point to up to 3 players. Update the CHOSEN reminders.
Check: did any player from last night's list die today (by execution)? If yes, all players from last night's list die tonight.
If no one from last night's list died today, only 1 of last night's 3 choices dies tonight (Storyteller's choice).
The new list is set for the following night.
examples: Night 2: the Warden's night 1 list had players A, B, C. Player B was executed today — all three (A, B, C) die tonight.
Night 2: none of A, B, C were executed today. One of them (the Storyteller chooses) dies tonight.
The Warden lists A, B, C. All three survive the day. Only A dies tonight. The Warden updates their list for tomorrow.
tips: Front-load your list with players you don't care about killing — if the town executes one, you get a triple kill for free.
Include yourself on the list occasionally — if you're executed, the triple kill fires. This creates a serious deterrent.
Pay attention to which players the town is likely to nominate. Listing them ensures maximum collateral damage on an execution.
If you can predict who gets executed, you can set up devastating night kills. Coordination with Minions who can influence nominations is valuable.
firstNightReminder: The Warden chooses up to 3 players. :reminder: :reminder: :reminder:
otherNightReminder: 1 or 2 chosen players die. :reminder: :reminder: The Warden chooses up to 3 players. :reminder: :reminder: :reminder:

name: Conspirator
ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die. If you didn’t vote today, choose an additional player: they die.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Conspirator kills methodically — and when they hold their tongue all day, the night grows twice as deadly.
summaryBullets: Each night (not the first), choose a player: they die. If you did not vote today, choose an additional player: they also die.
Not voting grants you a bonus kill — up to 2 kills per night.
The DID NOT VOTE reminder tracks your voting status each day.
Two DEAD reminders track both potential kills.
howToRun: Each night (not the first), wake the Conspirator. They point to a player — that player dies. Check the DID NOT VOTE reminder: if the Conspirator abstained from all votes today, they may point to a second player who also dies.
Reset the DID NOT VOTE reminder each dawn.
If the Conspirator voted at least once today, only one kill occurs.
examples: The Conspirator did not vote today. That night they kill both the Empath and the Slayer — two kills.
The Conspirator voted to execute the Monk. That night they can only kill one player.
The Conspirator abstained all day. The Demon is also killing tonight — three total deaths.
tips: Abstaining from votes is suspicious. Find cover stories — claim you forgot, claim you were strategically saving your vote, or coordinate with Minions to make it seem ordinary.
Save your double kill for critical moments. Eliminating two powerful information roles in one night is devastating.
On nights you can't afford two kills, vote openly to stay under cover — single kills are still highly effective.
If the town notices you never vote, they may suspect a Conspirator. Mix your voting pattern to avoid the tell.
otherNightReminder: The Conspirator chooses a player. :reminder: If the Conspirator did not vote today, they choose an additional player. :reminder:

name: Tyrant
ability: Each night*, choose 0 to 2 players: they die. Your choices cannot equal the number of deaths last night (max 2).
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: The Tyrant kills a different number of players each night.
summaryBullets: The Tyrant can choose to attack up to 2 players each night, but cannot attack as many players as died last night.
The Tyrant's ability takes all deaths last night into account, not only those caused by its own ability.
The Tyrant cannot attack more than 1 player if 2 or more players died last night.
The Tyrant can choose not to attack if at least 1 player died last night.
The Tyrant must attack at least 1 player if no one died last night.
The Tyrant can choose to attack dead players.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Tyrant. They either shake their head no or point at one or two players. The chosen players die in the order selected—mark them with [[DEAD]] reminder tokens.

If 2 or more players died last night, the Tyrant cannot choose two players tonight. If 1 player died last night, the Tyrant must choose 2 players or shake their head no. If no players died last night, the Tyrant must choose at least 1 player.
examples: The Tyrant chooses the Gossip and the Gambler, who are both protected by the Innkeeper. Nobody dies that night. The next night, the Tyrant must choose 1 or 2 players.

1 player died last night. Tonight, the Tyrant chooses the living Courtier and the dead Exorcist. The Courtier dies, and is the only one who dies tonight. The following night, the Tyrant must choose 0 or 2 players.
tips: The pattern restriction creates a predictability paradox — the town thinks they can predict your kill count, but you're always varying it.
0-kill nights are powerful misdirection. If the town expects kills and nobody dies, confusion ensues.
Coordinate the death count with Minions. If a Minion kills someone that night, your restriction is based on total deaths — plan accordingly.
The Tyrant is strongest when the town thinks they understand the pattern. Disrupt their assumptions by breaking expected sequences.
otherNightReminder: The Tyrant chooses 0, 1 or 2 players. :reminder: :reminder:
quote: What is the peasant's wrath to the Emperor's, which may kill hundreds upon thousands and bathe the land in blood.

name: Guhuo
ability: Each night*, choose a player: they die. Once per game, when a Minion dies by execution, you might gain their ability.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
tags: Single-Kill, Character Change, Once Per Game
lede: The Guhuo gains Minion abilities.
summaryBullets: When a Minion dies by execution, the Storyteller may decide to give the Guhuo that Minion's ability.
Executing a dead Minion won't grant the Guhuo an ability. Executing a living Minion who doesn't die won't grant the Guhuo an ability.
The Guhuo is not told when they have gained a Minion ability.
The Guhuo gains the ability that the Minion had when executed, even if that Minion later changes characters.
A Guhuo can only gain a Minion ability once per game.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Guhuo. They point at any player. The chosen player dies—mark them with the [[DEAD]] reminder.

If a Minion died by execution today and the [[ONCE]] reminder is not in the center of the Grimoire, the Guhuo might gain that Minion's ability. If so, place the Guhuo's [[HAS ABILITY]] reminder next to that Minion's character token: the Guhuo now has that Minion's ability, and will be awakened at night when that Minion would wake. Place the [[ONCE]] reminder in the center of the Grimoire.
examples: The Poisoner was executed and died. That night, the Guhuo wakes up to choose a player to poison, and later wakes up again to choose a player to kill.

The Devil's Advocate was executed and died. That night, the Pit-Hag transforms the Imp into the Guhuo. The Guhuo cannot gain the Devil's Advocate ability, as they were not in play when the Devil's Advocate died.

The Assassin was executed and died, and the Guhuo gained the Assassin ability. That night, the Guhuo killed the Barber and swapped characters with the Godfather. The next day, the Godfather was executed and died, and the new Guhuo cannot gain the Godfather ability.
tips: The prospect of gaining a Minion's ability incentivizes the town to not execute Minions — use this psychology.
If a Minion is about to be executed, signal to your team whether you want the ability. Some Minion abilities are worth gaining, others aren't.
A Guhuo with a Poisoner ability is extremely dangerous — double-poisoning plus kills each night.
The town should try to figure out if the Guhuo has gained an ability. Extra poisoning or unexpected effects may tip them off.
otherNightReminder: The Guhuo chooses a player. :reminder: The Guhuo might wake due to their Minion ability.
quote: At night, they feast on the souls of the dead, and steal the children to raise as their own.

name: Messenger
ability: There are no restrictions on abilities that require players to make a choice.
creator: Gstone Games
appearsIn: Festival of Lanterns — The Storm Is Coming
lede: Use the Messenger when there are new players who may be overwhelmed by the restrictions on some characters' abilities.
summaryBullets: The Messenger removes restrictions on abilities that require players to make a choice. Some characters may be too powerful without restrictions, so think carefully before deciding to use this Fabled.
tips: The Messenger removes restrictions for everyone — this can benefit evil as much as good. Know what restrictions are lifted for the evil team too.
Coordinate around the lifted restrictions. Good abilities that were previously constrained may now be far more powerful.
As the Storyteller, announce the Messenger clearly at the start — players need to know their full options.
Some restrictions are there for balance reasons. Removing them changes the game dynamics significantly — prepare for unexpected interactions.
quote: By the grace of heaven, the Emperor decrees: A general amnesty is granted throughout the land.

name: Don Quixote
ability: 1 player registers as a Demon to Townsfolk abilities, even if you are dead. You may privately learn who, then another player will.
creator: Taiyi
lede: Don Quixote has spent his whole life chasing the wrong targets, but he has never once given up.
summaryBullets: One player is marked as "him." Whenever a Townsfolk ability would determine that player's character or character type, that player registers as the Demon, and as a Demon character of the Storyteller's choice.
This applies even if the Don Quixote is dead. Dying does not end the false registration.
At any time while alive, the Don Quixote may privately visit the Storyteller to learn who "he" currently is. As soon as the Storyteller answers, "he" immediately moves to a different player.

The Don Quixote's ability only affects the abilities of Townsfolk characters. It does not affect the abilities of other characters (Outsiders, Minions, or Demons), and it does not affect the rules of the game.
Abilities that detect alignment, or that detect whether a player is a Minion, are not affected. "He" registers as the Demon character and the Demon character type — not as the evil alignment.
Any player can be "him": alive or dead, the Don Quixote himself, a Townsfolk, an Outsider, a Minion — even the actual Demon.
quote: "Shh... keep your voice down! What windmill? That is a giant! What goat? That is the Demon! ...Sancho! Charge with me!"

name: Cadenza
ability: If you have been executed, the Storyteller might follow your public commands from now on.
creator: Hystrex
lede: The game becomes the Cadenza's improvised solo.
summaryBullets: Once the Cadenza is executed, they can command the Storyteller to do anything. If the Storyteller agrees, they will do whatever the Cadenza told them.
Cadenza commands must be made in public. Private commands cannot be followed. A command is considered public if all players have the opportunity to hear it.
The Cadenza may make as many or as few commands as they wish. The Storyteller may follow as many or as few commands as they feel is fun for the players. The Cadenza may even make commands at night.

If the Cadenza is revived after being executed, they may still command the Storyteller. If the Cadenza was drunk or poisoned when they were executed, but is currently sober & healthy, they may still command the Storyteller.
howToRun: If the Cadenza is executed, mark them with the SOLO reminder. While this reminder is on the Cadenza, if they publicly command you to do something, you may follow their command. Make any mechanical adjustments to the game to match what the Cadenza wants. Use the various ? reminders as needed.
If you wish, you may say "Music to my ears" or something similar to indicate that a command has been followed. However, this is not required.
callout: Running the Cadenza can be tricky. Don't be afraid to take your time to consider whether or not to follow a command. If in doubt, you can always not follow a command, and the Cadenza can repeat the command later or make a weaker version of the command, if they want. However, keep in mind that Cadenza games are most fun then as many commands are followed as possible while maintaining some balance.
examples: Stick is the Cadenza who has been executed. Every day from then on, she publicly chooses a player and tells the Storyteller to poison them until dawn if they are a Townsfolk. The Storyteller poisons those players accordingly.

The Cadenza is executed on day 3. They command the Storyteller to make some true things that the Demon is mad about become false, from now on. The Barrkoad tells players that they think a Barrkoad is in play, and turns into the No Dashii tonight.

Bhamber, the Cadenza, is executed on day 1. For the majority of the game, he makes no commands, continuing to bluff as a good player. During nominations, when just 4 players live, he points to Foxx and says "mods, kill this man". Foxx dies.
quote: Nemmeno Dio può resistere alla melodia della mia musica! Also, you're flat.

jinxes.name: Plague Doctor
jinxes.text: If the Plague Doctor is executed & Storyteller gains the Cadenza ability, a Minion gains it instead & learns this.

name: Jack
ability: Each night*, you may choose a player: they die. If they do, all players learn a clue to your identity at dawn.
creator: DJ_DJ_DJ
lede: Jack can wreak as much havoc as they like, but can't stop themselves from leaving clues.
summaryBullets: Like a demon, Jack can kill a player each night. However, if they keep killing they will almost certainly be caught.
Clues are spoken publicly to all players, so it's obvious when a Jack is killing.
The Jack may choose to not kill at all, thus concealing their identity.
Clues may be any statement at all that the Storyteller thinks will help town find the Jack.
In certain situations clues may be more precise, or more broad, depending on what the Storyteller deems balanced.
Clues may be game related, or not.
howToRun: Each night except the first, wake the Jack. They either shake their head no or point at any player. Put the Jack to sleep.
If the Jack chose a player, that player dies— mark them with the [[RIP]] reminder. If the Jack chose a player & they died, announce a clue to the group that dawn.
callout: A good rule of thumb is for the first clue to narrow half of town as Jack, the second 1 of 3 people & the third reveal or almost reveal the Jack outright.
examples: Steve the Jack kills a player on the second night. At dawn, the Storyteller announces "Jack voted yesterday". Around half of the players voted yesterday.
Steve the Jack kills another player on the third night. At dawn, the Storyteller announces "Jack is wearing a hat". 3 players, including Steve are wearing hats.
Steve the Jack kills yet another player on the fourth night. At dawn, the Storyteller announces "Jacks name starts with ST and ends with EVE. Steve is executed.
tips: You can kill all you like! Go wild! It's ok if you are found & executed, at least its not your demon!
Stay silent. Sneak around. You are much more valuable late game, when your clues matter less.
Your clues are a distraction. They interrupt the good teams solving and flow. When they are looking for you, they aren't looking for the demon.
Try to misdirect your clues. If the clue is "Jack claimed to be an outsider yesterday " after you were loudly claiming one, insist it couldn't possibly be you, because it's so obvious!
Don't bother planning for the long term. You'll probably be outed evil later in the game, so lie like crazy in the short term to waste time and executions!
Talk as much as possible. The better targets you find the more valuable you are, and you don't want the good team noticing you only talking to your evil team if they find you out later.
quote: Dear Boss,
otherNightReminder: Wake the Jack. They either shake their head no or point at any player. Put the Jack to sleep.

name: Vampire
ability: You wake for Demon info instead of Minion info. You know which Minion you added. [+1 good Minion who thinks they are evil]
quote: I will be welcomed, and you shall be damned.
flavor: I will be welcomed, and you shall be damned.
lede: The Vampire hides in the shadows to keep their thrall in the dark.
firstNightReminder: Do not wake the Vampire during Minion info. Wake the Vampire during Demon info and show 3 not-in-play good character tokens. Put the Demon to sleep, then point the Vampire towards the good Minion.
summaryBullets: The Minions don’t learn that there is a Vampire. They learn who the Demon is, as usual, and the expected number of Minions will wake.
The Vampire wakes with the Demon instead, learning the other Minions and bluffs with them.
One Minion is good. They register as good, & win with good, but they think they’re evil.
They still have their Minion ability, and it works like normal.
howToRun: While setting up the bag, replace a Townsfolk token with an extra Minion token. Upon receiving the tokens back, mark one of them with the [[THRALL]] reminder (not the Vampire), and turn it upside down to indicate that player is good.
During the “Minion Info” step of the first night, do not wake the Vampire. Run this step as usual, not indicating which player is the Vampire.

During the “Demon Info” step of the first night, wake the Vampire as well as the Demon. Let them acknowledge each other. Indicate to each who the Minions are. Show each of them three characters which are not in play. (You can show them different characters, if you want!)
Put the Demon to sleep. Show the Vampire the player marked with the [[THRALL]] reminder. Put the Vampire to sleep.
examples: Zoe the Fearmonger and Yolanda the Poisoner wake together on the first night, and learn that Alex is the Demon. Then, Alex the Imp and Xavier the Vampire wake together and learn that Zoe and Yolanda are the Minions, and each learn a set of bluffs. The Vampire learns that Yolanda is the good Minion they added.

Melvin is the good Devil's Advocate and thinks he’s evil. Melvin nominates a player that day, and neither the Vampire nor the other Minion does. The Town Crier still learns that a Minion nominated today.

All three Village Idiots choose Nora, the good Baron. Two learn good, and the drunk Village Idiot learns evil. Hearing this, Nora publicly claims to be the good Minion and nominates Lexi, her Demon. Lexi is executed and dies. Good wins, including Nora.

jinxes.reason: An Alchemist-Vampire has no Vampire ability & the Vampire is in play.
The Engineer cannot create a Vampire.
The Vampire does not wake to change characters using the Hatter. Players cannot choose to become the Vampire using the Hatter.
The Storyteller cannot gain the Vampire ability.
If the Vampire makes a good Evil Twin, they register as evil to their own ability.
If the Vampire makes a good Spy, that player sees themselves as evil in the Grimoire, & the Vampire as good & as a good character. The Vampire learns which character they were seen as.
The Vampire learns which player the Summoner chooses.
If the Vampire makes a good Vizier, they register as evil to their own ability.
If the Vampire makes a good Widow, that player sees themselves as evil in the Grimoire, & the Vampire as good & as a good character. The Vampire learns which character they were seen as.
The Wraith cannot wake during Demon info. The good Minion added by the Vampire registers as evil to the Wraith.
If Kazali makes a Vampire, they choose another player to become one of their Minions. Then, they choose which of the non-Vampire Minions is secretly good.
The Vampire doesn't wake to choose who babysits Lil' Monsta.
A Vampire can only be placed at either end of the line & cannot neighbour the Demon. The good Minion is elsewhere in the line.
If the Vampire adds a good Chancellor, they register as evil to their own ability.
